Explanation: The clues point to Alexandra Walsham, a historian specializing in early modern Europe, particularly seventeenth-century England and France. She is affiliated with Trinity College, Cambridge, as shown in [50629]. The 2012 book and the reference to chapters written in Cambridge align with her academic base. Fitzwilliam College, University of Cambridge, is also mentioned in a 2020 PhD thesis on identity and morality in early modern Europe [9838]. Trinity College, Cambridge, was founded before 1800 and is part of the University of Cambridge.
